<!-- Relic Infobox is the template that controls the infobox of just your relic and is mandatory. This name will be typed with spaces, and the correct capitalization.

Keyword Infobox controls the infobox of additional effects like Vigor or unique buffs like Pen Nib. If your relic does not have any that need to be shown, remove it entirely.

A lot of keywords aren't in the database yet, so feel free to ask any of us (User:Riscuit, User:Vesper-arch, or User:Aryemlights or just ping us on Discord) to add them.

Some relics may need access to card lists, like Toolbox, example "... choose 1 of 3 random Colorless cards ...".
Use eg, {{QueryLink|Cards|color:Colorless|Colorless|2}} to create the link that will only list out Colorless cards. Remember to add |2 as the last parameter to make it link to the Slay the Spire 2 pages.

Some relics (Mark of Pain off the top of my head) may need an extra Infobox for a card or keyword. Use {{Card Infobox|CARDNAME|2}} and/or {{Keyword Infobox|KEYWORDNAME|2}} right after {{Relic Infobox|Pael's Wing|2}}, without spaces.
-->

== Sources ==
After fighting a {{2|Bosses|Boss}}, can be offered by {{2|Pael}}.


== Spawn Restrictions==
<!--
Find out what restrictions your relic has, at Relics#Restrictions.

IF YOUR RELIC HAS NO SPAWN RESTRICTIONS, AND ISN'T AN EVENT RELIC, SHOP RELIC OR BOSS RELIC:
No spawn restrictions.

Remove this section entirely if it's one of those 3.

---------
OTHERWISE UNCOMMENT BELOW AS NECESSARY, IN BULLET POINTS IF THERE'S MORE THAN 1 RESTRICTION:
---------


IF YOUR RELIC HAS FLOOR RESTRICTIONS:
Can only be obtained below floor X.

IF YOUR RELIC HAS SHOP RESTRICTIONS:
Cannot be bought from the {{2|Merchant}}.

IF YOUR RELIC IS WHETSTONE (NOT WAR PAINT):
Cannot be obtained from Calling Bell and "Obtain Relic" events, that drop the relic without it being a combat reward. This means, for example, {{R|Pael's Wing||2}} can be obtained from {{E|Dead Adventurer}} if the Elite is beaten, but cannot be obtained if it is found from the {{Frame|Search}} option.

IF IT'S SHOVEL or GIRYA:
* {{R|Black Star}}'s extra relic can never drop {{R|Pael's Wing||2}}.


-->

== Interactions ==

=== Order ===
<!-- Include any notable ordering here, example Dead Branch and Dark Embrace. If this is a card with Weak/Vuln + Strength/Vigor etc kind of ordering (example Wreath of Flame), uncomment the next comment. 
Note: Additive: Strength, Vigor, anything with +1
      Multiplicative: Weak, Vuln, Wrath, Divinity, Phantasmal's double damage, Lock-On, anything with %
-->

<!--
When calculating damage dealt, all the additive bonuses are added first, and then multiplicative bonuses are multiplied. The final result is rounded down. '''Pael's Wing''''s {{KW|BONUSNAME}} is an additive/multiplicative bonus, (you can remove this part if irrelevant) and stacks with (most common synergy).
-->

== Trivia ==
<!-- Include any relevant trivia here, including confirmed references by the devs (Example, The Specimen being a Dorohedoro reference, and different internal names (Example, Gold-Plated Cables being called Cables internally). Please do add sources for any trivia you add here!

Do not add subjective opinion here like "xyz relic is considered bad".
-->

<!--
This is all the references to relics that the devs have confirmed:
	Art of War: References the Art of War by Sun Tzu.
	Black Star: References a song by David Bowie.
	Blue Candle: References Blue Candle, an item from The Legend of Zelda.
	Bowling Bash: References a Knight skill from the game Ragnarok Online.
	Brimstone: References the "Brimstone" item from the Binding of Isaac (symbol is hidden in the art).
	Calling Bell: References the Beckoning Bell from Bloodborne.
	Centennial Puzzle: References the Millenium Puzzle from Yu-gi-oh.
	Charon's Ashes: References Charon of Greek Mythology.
	Chemical X: References a chemical of the same name from Power Puff Girls.
	Dead Branch: References the Dead Branch from Ragnarok Online.
	Dolly's Mirror: References Dolly, the first cloned sheep.
	Du-Vu Doll: References Voodoo doll.
	Enchiridion: References the Enchirdion, specifically the one from Adventure Time.
	Fossilized Helix: References Helix Fossil from Pokemon.
	Gold-Plated Cables: Flavor text references plating cables with gold to improve them (hint: it doesn't).
	Golden Idol/Bloody Idol: References the Golden Idol from Spelunky.
	Hand Drill: Visually references Gurren Lagann's comically oversized drills.
	Happy Flower: References the Sunflower from Plants vs Zombies.
	Inserter: This is the Inserter device from Factorio! The image is a yellow Inserter.
	Lee's Waffle: References Lee, friend of the developer- he had a waffle machine at work.
	Melange: Refers to the spice, from the Dune series.
	Necronomicon: References the Necronomicon from Evil Dead.
	Nilry's Codex: References Codex. A tabletop game by Sirlin (Nilry is Sirlin backwards), a game designer.
	Paper Krane: This is an origami paper crane.
	Paper Phrog: This is an origami paper frog.
	Prismatic Shard: Item in Stardew Valley.
	Question Card: References the "? Card" from the Binding of Isaac.
	Shovel: Visually references the shovel used by Shovel Knight.
	The Specimen: Visually references Kaiman from Dorohedoro.
	Toxic Egg: Visually references Alien.
	Violet Lotus: A purple version of Black Lotus, a Magic: The Gathering card.
	Void Essence: Item in Stardew Valley.
	Wing Boots: References Boots of Travel from Dota.
-->
